data.description:
## Incident Overview

**Scenario Type**: Pre-existing test failure (unrelated to recent session work)
**Source**: Discovered during /dso:end-session test gate run on 2026-04-27.

**Expected Behavior**: tests/scripts/test_bridge_outbound.py::test_write_sync_event_timestamp_is_nanosecond_scale asserts that emitted sync-event timestamps are at nanosecond scale.

**Actual Behavior**: Assertion fails on clean origin/main. Verified pre-existing via 'git stash' baseline.

**Reproduction**: python3 -m pytest tests/scripts/test_bridge_outbound.py::test_write_sync_event_timestamp_is_nanosecond_scale

**Related**: Possibly related to bridge sync issues tracked in 44a0-3a13, but symptom (timestamp scale) is distinct enough to warrant separate investigation.

**Investigation Plan**: Run the failing test with -v to see the actual timestamp value; check whether the producer (likely _write_sync_event in ticket_graph or sync.py) is using time.time() vs time.time_ns(); align producer or test.
data.title: [bridge-outbound]: test_write_sync_event_timestamp_is_nanosecond_scale -> assertion failure on clean main
